title: pyrus aucuparia subsp. praemorsa
intro: Pyrus aucuparia subsp. praemorsa is a subspecies of the rowan tree, commonly found in Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, yellowish-green berries and distinctive thorny branches. The berries of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Pyrus aucuparia subsp. praemorsa berries include their ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function, providing relief from colds, sore throats, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
